name: Corcoya desde Badolatos — circular por el río Genil y La Vía Verde Subbètica
region_intro: Mountain biking around Badolatosa offers varied terrain within the picturesque Genil Valley, characterized by the meandering Genil River and significant protected wetlands. The landscape includes undulating paths and dirt tracks, providing a mix of challenges for mountain bikers. Routes often trace the Genil River and pass by the Malpasillo and Cordobilla reservoirs, offering waterside views. This region provides a natural backdrop for exploring the Andalusian countryside on two wheels. Best mountain bike trails around Badolatosa The most popular mountain bike trail is Puente Genil, Jauja, Pantano de Cordobilla – ruta circular, a 33.4 miles (53.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area around the Cordobilla Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Castle Anzur from Puente Genil - circular via the Cordobilla Reservoir, a moderate 36.5 miles (58.7 km) path. This route offers views of the Cordobilla Reservoir and surrounding landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Corcoya desde Badolatos — circular por el río Genil y La Vía Verde Subbètica, a 16.2 miles (26.1 km) trail leading through the Genil River valley, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Mountain biking around Badolatosa is defined by the Genil River, the Cordobilla and Malpasillo reservoirs, and varied terrain including dirt tracks and undulating paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging sections. The routes in Badolatosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Badolatosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Badolatosa?
A: There are over 120 mountain bike trails available around Badolatosa. These include a variety of routes, with 15 easy trails, 98 moderate trails, and 11 difficult trails, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Badolatosa?
A: Mountain biking around Badolatosa offers varied terrain within the picturesque Genil Valley. You can expect undulating paths, dirt tracks, and sections that trace the Genil River, as well as routes passing by the Malpasillo and Cordobilla reservoirs. This mix provides both challenging and more accessible sections.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in Badolatosa?
A: Yes, Badolatosa offers several easy mountain bike trails suitable for families. These routes often feature flatter, easily accessible paths, allowing for a pleasant experience for all skill levels. While specific family-designated routes aren't detailed, the presence of 15 easy trails suggests suitable options.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around Badolatosa?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986644555/puente-genil-jauja-pantano-de-cordobilla-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Genil, Jauja, Pantano de Cordobilla – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate trail exploring the area around the Cordobilla Reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271551478/corcoya-desde-badolatos-circular-por-el-rio-genil-y-la-via-verde-subbetica" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corcoya desde Badolatos — circular por el río Genil y La Vía Verde Subbètica&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Genil River valley.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often feature scenic views of the Genil River, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genil River Meander&lt;/a&gt;. You can also enjoy waterside views around the Malpasillo and Cordobilla reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5394288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Malpasillo Dam and Genil River&lt;/a&gt; highlight offers a great vantage point.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the region has historical connections. While not directly on all MTB trails, Badolatosa is linked to the famous outlaw José María "El Tempranillo," and you can explore caves used by him. The "Ruta del Tempranillo" allows for exploration of natural surroundings alongside historical sites. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6039029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purísima Inmaculada Concepción Parish, Alameda&lt;/a&gt; is a notable religious building nearby.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Badolatosa?
A: The mountain bike trails around Badolatosa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the Genil Valley, and the opportunity to explore the Andalusian countryside on two wheels.

Q: Are there any advanced or difficult mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 11 difficult mountain bike trails around Badolatosa. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31564151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Iron Bridge – San Marcos Bridge loop from Puente Genil&lt;/a&gt;, which is a demanding 93.2 km (57.9 miles) trail.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Badolatosa?
A: Andalusia generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and pleasant weather for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if riding during this season.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by the Cordobilla Reservoir?
A: Yes, the Cordobilla Reservoir is a prominent feature in many routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271597835/castle-anzur-from-puente-genil-circular-via-the-cordobilla-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle Anzur from Puente Genil - circular via the Cordobilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate path offering views of the reservoir and surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there any greenways or dedicated cycle paths in the area?
A: Yes, the region features parts of the Vía Verde. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1548356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jauja – Subbética Greenway Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4737330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Subbética Greenway&lt;/a&gt; offer dedicated paths that can be incorporated into mountain bike routes, providing smoother sections for riding.
tips: It is located on the outskirts of the municipality and was built between 1544 and 1620. It is a Mozarabic-style cave church in which the breadth of its façade stands out, making it one of the few monuments of these characteristics in Andalusia. .

From this point we can see the dam on the Genil river and the town of Badolatosa on our left. Going down the dirt road we will arrive at the famous meander of the Genil river (it is a 15-minute walk).

The Greenway of the Subbètica runs through the southern part of the province of Córdoba. This infrastructure is in perfect condition for the transit with our bicycles and the absence of motor vehicles makes it very attractive. The slope is very slight, being suitable for any type of user.
The environment is marked by olive groves and vineyards. It has old stations enabled as restaurants.
Without a doubt, a beautiful place to enjoy cycling.
